channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Top senator criticizes Pentagon 'mid-level' leaders on troop level plan in Europe
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The chairman of the Senate Armed Services Committee criticized Pentagon “mid-level” leadership for what he said Thursday was a misguided plan to reduce the number of U.S. troops based in Europe.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Other DFL statewide officeholders break with Walz on return-to-office requirement
item.description: The attorney general, secretary of state and state auditor said they would keep existing telework rules in place because they are independent offices.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump moves to fire several national security officials deemed insufficiently loyal, AP sources say
item.description: President Donald Trump has moved to fire several White House National Security Council officials after he was urged by far-right activist Laura Loomer to purge staffers she deemed insufficiently committed to his agenda.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Aamer Madhani And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump admin searching for countries to accept migrants: Report
item.description: It is offering financial incentives as well as political benefits to countries in Africa, Asia and Eastern Europe.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, NewsNation

item.title: Trump admin moves to more easily fire federal workers at 2 agencies: report
item.description: The White House is reportedly moving to reclassify federal workers at the Department of Energy and elsewhere in order to remove their job protections.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SCOOP: Congress barrels toward showdown over Biden-era rule letting California ban gas cars
item.description: A showdown is brewing between Congress and nonpartisan federal offices over lawmakers' power to repeal a Biden-era policy on electric vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DeSantis announces support as Florida Senate takes up 'chemtrails' bill
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis expressed his support for a bill banning what he called weather modification "nonsense" before it hits the Senate floor on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ted Cruz clashes with key Democrat over 'second phase of lawfare' through federal judges' orders
item.description: Ted Cruz had a heated exchange with Amy Klobuchar during a hearing looking at the uptick in nationwide orders from district judges in Trump's second administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna clashes with GOP House leaders, plans tele-town hall
item.description: The Pinellas County Republican is pushing to allow new parents the ability to vote by proxy, or remotely, for up to 12 weeks after having a baby.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: SCOOP: White House rallies House GOP on Trump tariff plan in secret call
item.description: The White House held a call with House Republican aides rallying them around Trump's tariff plan, Fox News Digital was told.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Social Security's acting leader faces calls to resign over decision to cut Maine contracts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Social Security Administration's acting commissioner is facing calls to resign after he issued an order — which was quickly rescinded — that would have required Maine parents to register their newborns for Social Security numbers at a federal office rather than the hospital.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ACLU sues Trump administration over NIH grant cancellations
item.description: The lawsuit, filed in Massachusetts district court, said there was no “scientifically-valid explanation or cause” for NIH to cancel the research projects.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from Tuesday’s elections, including bad news for Elon Musk
item.description: Democrats can take yesterday’s elections as one of the first bits of good news they’ve had in months. But there are lots of issues that are still center-right, including immigration.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Judge dismisses corruption case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge dismissed New York City Mayor Eric Adams’ corruption case on Wednesday, agreeing it was the only practical outcome while blasting the Justice Department’s “troubling” rationale for wanting the charges thrown out — namely so the Democrat could help President Donald Tru
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Charges dismissed against Illinois man accused of assault by Rep. Nancy Mace
item.description: U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace claimed the assault came in response to her efforts to bar transgender women from using women's restrooms on federal property.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Brams, NewsNation

item.title: Trump restoring millions in family planning funds for two states
item.description: The Trump administration is restoring millions of dollars in Title X funds to Oklahoma and Tennessee after the Biden administration chose to withhold those funds because both states failed to comply with program rules.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: Trump gives GOP senators his support as they charge ahead on tax package and spending cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican senators unveiled a budget proposal Wednesday that's central to President Donald Trump's domestic agenda of tax breaks, spending cuts and border security, but they're delaying some of the most difficult decisions, including how to pay for the multitrillion-dollar package
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former aide slams California Democrat in video announcing primary campaign
item.description: Jake Rakov, a former aide to Rep. Brad Sherman (D-Calif.), on Wednesday announced he is launching a primary challenge against his onetime boss.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Once common, now unusual: Conservative candidate publicly quashes shouts that the other side cheated
item.description: PEWAUKEE, Wis. (AP) — As the first news outlets began calling the Wisconsin Supreme Court election for the liberal candidate Susan Crawford, her opponent called her — to concede.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Charges dismissed against Illinois man accused of assault by Rep. Nancy Mace
item.description: Federal prosecutors moved to dismiss charges Tuesday against a man U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) accused of assaulting her during an event at the Capitol last year.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Brams, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada Democrats outnumber Republicans by just 2 voters amid statewide switch to nonpartisan
item.description: After Nevada Republicans surpassed their Democratic counterparts in voter registration for the first time in nearly 20 years, Democrats have once again taken over as the state's top party -- with a voter registration lead of two.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Scientists fear impact of National Weather Service cuts
item.description: As the summer hurricane season looms, so too do cuts to the federal workforce, including the National Weather Service.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tariff plan could cost households thousands: New analysis
item.description: A new analysis from The Budget Lab at Yale shows how much a broad tariff plan could cost U.S. households.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic-backed Susan Crawford wins Wisconsin Supreme Court seat, cementing liberal majority
item.description: Susan Crawford, a Dane County judge who led legal fights to protect union power and abortion rights and to oppose voter ID, defeated Republican-backed Brad Schimel in a race that broke records for spending.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: GOP retains two House seats in Florida, Democrats claim 'historic' improvement
item.description: Republicans won special elections in two Florida Congressional districts. The margins of victory in the heavily-Republican districts were significantly narrower than in November.
item.dc:creator: Greg Allen, MPR News

item.title: New York City mayoral candidates vow to address rising antisemitism
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The leading candidates for New York City mayor say they have plans to fight a rise in antisemitism. One in 10 New Yorkers is Jewish, making it the largest Jewish population outside of Israel. Former Gov.
item.dc:creator: Anthony DiLorenzo, PIX 11

item.title: California lawmakers reject bills to ban trans athletes' participation in girls sports
item.description: California lawmakers won't change state policies allowing transgender kids and teens to play on sports teams consistent with their gender identities.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: House cancels rest of votes for week after GOP floor rebellion
item.description: House Republican leaders on Tuesday canceled votes for the rest of the week after a band of GOP lawmakers staged a rebellion on the floor, bringing legislative action to a screeching halt. House Majority Whip Tom Emmer (R-Minn.) informed lawmakers the next vote in the House would be Monday evening.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court seems ready to let religious groups opt out of unemployment compensation laws
item.description: The case was brought by a chapter of Catholic Charities in Wisconsin, which says that it should be able to opt out of the mandatory state unemployment compensation system.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Missouri pastor gets deportation notice for sponsored son from Haiti
item.description: "Do we welcome immigrants in, if they come legally, and are pursing the citizenship process? Is that true or is that not?"
item.dc:creator: Hannah King, Fox 5

item.title: Green card holders, travelers caught in Trump’s immigration crackdown
item.description: A lawful permanent resident who has lived in the U.S. for 50 years was detained because of a decades-old conviction amid tougher immigration enforcement at airports and border crossings.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose, MPR News

item.title: Election Day: Suburban voters head to the polls
item.description: Several elections will take place in Chicago-area suburbs on Tuesday, including a notable one in Dolton where incumbent Tiffany Henyard will run against Jason House, who won the primary election in February.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, WGN 9

item.title: Watch: House committee considering a cap on school voucher spending, increases to public school funding
item.description: The House Public Education Committee released new proposals for creating education savings accounts and determining how much state funds public districts get.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jaden Edison, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘not joking’ about a third term. Republicans insist he is
item.description: Congressional Republicans are brushing off President Trump’s latest comments about seeking a third term, downplaying them as a joke designed to needle the media even as the president insists he is serious.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minnesota insurance costs could continue to rise under new Trump tariffs
item.description: Tariffs are taxes on goods imported from other countries, but these new taxes are not just impacting the prices of our T-shirts and televisions. Tariffs may even lead to higher insurance rates here at home.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crann and Gretchen Brown, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin Supreme Court race draws national attention, record out-of-state money
item.description: Zac Schultz, a reporter with PBS Wisconsin’s “Here & Now” program, has been closely covering the race and joined MPR News host Cathy Wurzer on Morning Edition with the highlights.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Gov. DeSantis proposes $1K rebate checks to cover Florida homeowners' property tax
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis announced a proposal on Monday that would give Florida homeowners a rebate averaging $1,000 for each homesteaded property in the state.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC
